hero.title: Explore the product catalog behind Patience AI
hero.description: Product-led solutions designed to show real value, route requests cleanly, and support every stage of evaluation.

products.name: Barrister
products.shortTagline: End-to-end case management for law firms — plus Find a Barrister, a public directory where clients search lawyers across India, chat, video-call, and book consultations.
products.summary: A comprehensive digital case management platform for law firms — now with Find a Barrister, a public directory that connects clients to legal help. Barrister brings case tracking, document management, payment recording, automated court reminders, and dual admin/advocate portals into a single secure system, while Find a Barrister lets anyone search lawyers and firms across India, start a live chat, jump into an instant video consultation, and book a slot — so firms spend less time on paperwork and win more clients.
benefits: Find a Barrister — a public directory to search lawyers and firms across India by state, city, locality, and PIN, with GPS-based auto-detection of your city.
Connect instantly — live 1:1 chat with any lawyer (sign in with Google or email OTP) and one-click private video consultations.
Public lawyer and firm profiles with photo, title, expertise, and bio, plus consultation booking against real-time availability slots.
Full case lifecycle tracking with status, court details, hearing dates, fees, and advocate assignment in one place.
Dual portals — a full-control admin portal and a focused advocate portal for assigned cases, documents, and payments.
Centralized document management with per-case organization, secure uploads, and access auditing.
Payment tracking across cash, cheque, NEFT, UPI, card, and DD with auto-generated receipts and reconciliation.
Automated email reminders for court appearances and case updates, with professional PDF case and receipt documents.
technologies: Next.js 15 and React 19 frontend with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM for durable, optimized data
NextAuth.js JWT authentication with OTP-based password reset
Brevo email integration with pdf-lib document generation
Secure role-based access with advocate access logging
products.audience: Law firms, advocates, and legal teams that need organized case tracking, documents, and client communication — and clients across India looking to find a lawyer, chat, and book a consultation
products.privacyTone: Security and compliance focused; JWT auth, hashed credentials, and access logging

products.name: SoNex
products.shortTagline: Room-aware adaptive volume — on-device ML that listens to the room, ducks media when someone talks, boosts it in noise, and learns your home over time.
products.summary: SoNex is a privacy-first Android system that automatically adjusts media volume across your phone, paired Android TV, Bluetooth speakers, and Chromecast based on what's happening in the room. When someone talks, volume ducks. When it gets noisy, volume rises. All audio is processed on-device using Silero VAD and YAMNet — nothing leaves the phone without explicit consent. Pair phone and TV with a 4-digit code over Wi-Fi, calibrate once, and SoNex handles the rest. A server-side learning loop trains per-home models from consented data and pushes them OTA — no app update needed. Voice control in English and Hindi lets you say 'SoNex, lower volume' hands-free.
benefits: Automatic speech detection — on-device Silero VAD and YAMNet classify every 30ms audio frame as speech, noise, or quiet, with an energy-heuristic fallback if models are absent.
Multi-device ducking — one command interface fans volume changes to the phone speaker, paired Android TV, Bluetooth media, and Cast devices simultaneously.
Privacy-first by design — all audio processed on-device, no data leaves without per-purpose consent toggles (all OFF by default), DPDP Act 2023 and GDPR compliant.
Per-home OTA learning — a server-side training loop turns consented events and user overrides into per-home models pushed over the air, so SoNex adapts to each household without an app update.
Phone-call awareness — detects active calls, forces ducking on all outputs, and restores only after the call ends and the room is quiet.
Voice control in English and Hindi — wake-word 'SoNex' plus on-device Vosk speech recognition for hands-free volume, stop, and play commands.
Guided calibration — a 3-step wizard captures silence floor, media-only baseline, and media+talk, then creates named profiles for different rooms.
technologies: Android (Kotlin) with on-device Silero VAD and YAMNet ML models
Android TV companion app with Wi-Fi pairing over local network
On-device Vosk speech recognition for wake-word and voice commands
Server-side OTA model training loop with per-home personalization
DPDP Act 2023 and GDPR compliant consent-gated data architecture
products.audience: Households, home-theater enthusiasts, and accessibility-focused users who want intelligent, automatic volume management across all their devices
products.privacyTone: Privacy is a hard constraint — on-device processing, consent-gated data sharing, and full DPDP/GDPR compliance

products.name: CrackXam
products.shortTagline: Fast, adaptive exam prep with intelligent practice, feedback, and collaboration.
products.summary: An intelligent exam-prep platform delivering timed practice, instant feedback, and personalized recommendations for focused learning.
benefits: AI-augmented recommendations and smart content filtering to surface the most relevant lessons.
Timed practice sessions with instant scoring and progress tracking to accelerate skill building.
Real-time collaboration and seamless sync across devices for group study and continuity.
Mobile-first, low-bandwidth delivery to ensure consistent performance in constrained networks.
Privacy-first controls and secure data handling for trusted learning experiences.
technologies: Modern JavaScript frontend with mobile-first delivery
Scalable cloud backend for secure APIs and session handling
Real-time sync layer for live collaboration
Edge or on-device ML for personalized recommendations
Durable cloud storage with offline resume support
products.audience: Learners and institutions seeking a performance-focused, AI-enhanced exam preparation solution
products.privacyTone: Privacy and security focused; enterprise controls available

products.name: Nexus Exchange
products.shortTagline: Smart reference rate management with real-time updates, bulk processing, and secure approvals.
products.summary: A reference rate management platform that helps financial teams track, update, and approve currency exchange rates in real time. Nexus Exchange acts as a single control center for live rates, bulk uploads, and governed approvals — built for banks, money transfer companies, and global businesses managing multi-currency operations.
benefits: Automatic real-time rate collection keeps every currency pair current without manual lookups.
High-speed bulk processing lets teams upload hundreds of rates from Excel or CSV with live progress tracking.
Built-in admin approval workflow ensures every rate change is reviewed before going live.
Granular access control with instant role updates protects sensitive financial operations.
Modern, distraction-free dashboard designed for clarity, speed, and operational confidence.
technologies: Real-time rate ingestion and live updates
Bulk Excel and CSV processing with progress tracking
Admin approval workflow and audit trail
Role-based access with instant permission updates
Cloud-ready, secure deployment for financial operations
products.audience: Banks, money transfer companies, currency exchanges, and international businesses managing multi-currency operations
products.privacyTone: Enterprise-grade security with governed approvals and audit trails

products.name: AI Email SaaS
products.shortTagline: Enterprise email automation with secure rules, context-aware drafting, and actionable analytics.
products.summary: Enterprise AI email automation that produces context-aware drafts, enforces templates and policies, and provides actionable analytics for operations teams.
benefits: Production-grade AI generates context-aware, brand-aligned drafts while respecting templates and business rules.
Template-driven personalization at scale keeps tone, compliance, and brand intact across high volumes.
Admin analytics and controls let teams tune AI behavior and measure business impact.
Low-latency delivery pipeline designed for reliability and auditability.
Secure integration and observability for compliance and operational transparency.
technologies: Production AI model integration for generation and classification
Asynchronous backend paired with a reactive frontend
Ingestion to delivery pipeline with monitoring and retries
Scalable cloud deployment and observability for enterprise SLAs
Secure, auditable data handling and admin controls
products.audience: Operations, marketing, and customer-facing teams looking to scale compliant, high-quality email workflows

detailPages.title: Features
detailPages.description: The Patience AI platform brings GenAI agents, retrieval, voice, and orchestration together into a governed surface built for Indian mid-market and global teams.
points: Custom GenAI agents grounded in your data with tool-use, function calling, and policy guardrails for finance, healthcare, and SaaS workflows.
Production-grade Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) with hybrid search, reranking, and source citations across PDFs, knowledge bases, and CRMs.
Voice AI with sub-700ms latency, barge-in support, and multilingual coverage for English, Hindi, Marathi, Tamil, Telugu, and Kannada.
Visual workflow automation in the spirit of n8n with 400+ triggers, branching, retries, and human-in-the-loop approval steps.
Omnichannel customer support across WhatsApp Business, web chat, voice, email, and Instagram DMs with unified conversation history.
Built-in analytics: deflection rate, containment, CSAT, average handle time, intent drift, and token-cost dashboards per workflow.
Role-based access, audit logs, PII redaction, prompt versioning, and evaluation harnesses for safe iteration.

detailPages.title: Integrations
detailPages.description: Patience AI plugs into the tools Indian and global teams already run on — messaging, CRMs, helpdesks, payments, and productivity suites.
points: Messaging: Slack, Microsoft Teams, WhatsApp Business Cloud API, Instagram Direct, and Telegram.
Telephony and voice: Twilio Programmable Voice, Exotel, Plivo, and SIP trunks for Indian carriers.
Workflow orchestration: n8n, Zapier, and Make for hybrid low-code automation alongside our native runtime.
CRM: Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM, and Freshsales with two-way sync of conversations and lead scores.
Helpdesk: Zendesk, Freshdesk, Intercom, and Front for ticket creation, escalation, and macro suggestions.
Productivity: Notion, Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Sheets), and Microsoft 365 (Outlook, OneDrive, Teams).
Payments and billing: Stripe, Razorpay, and Cashfree for in-conversation checkout and subscription updates.
Data and storage: Neon Postgres, Supabase, S3, Google Cloud Storage, and Snowflake for retrieval and analytics.

detailPages.title: Pricing
detailPages.description: We scope pricing around what you actually need — channels, conversation volume, and integrations — so the plan fits your use case instead of a one-size tier. Talk to our team and we'll put together a quote. Pilots start in days, not quarters.
points: Starter — for early-stage SaaS and D2C teams: a single GenAI agent across web and WhatsApp, RAG over your core documents, and email support to get you live quickly.
Growth — for scaling teams: multiple agents across all messaging channels, a voice AI add-on, workflow automation, CRM and helpdesk integrations, priority support, and a dedicated success manager.

Enterprise — for high-volume and regulated teams: dedicated voice infrastructure with custom latency SLAs, on-prem or VPC deployment, DPDP and SOC 2 documentation, custom integrations, 24x7 support, and a named solutions architect.
Pilot program: a short, scoped pilot with success criteria agreed upfront, so you can prove value before committing.
Add-ons available on request: additional voice minutes, extra RAG storage, and premium model routing — sized to your usage and included in your quote.
Every plan includes audit logs, role-based access, PII redaction, and India data residency. Contact us for a tailored quote.

detailPages.title: Changelog
detailPages.description: What we shipped recently. Dates in IST. Hotfixes and small UX tweaks are batched into weekly notes.
points: 2026-06-03 — Voice AI v2.4: rolled out barge-in latency improvements (median 420ms, p95 680ms), added Marathi and Kannada TTS voices, and shipped a new diarization pipeline for multi-speaker calls.
2026-05-19 — Agentic RAG: introduced multi-hop retrieval with reranking via bge-reranker-v2, added per-source citation badges in the chat UI, and exposed retrieval traces for evaluation.
2026-04-28 — Groq model routing: added automatic failover across Groq, Anthropic, and OpenAI based on latency budgets and cost ceilings. Average response time on the support agent dropped 38%.
2026-04-07 — n8n-compatible workflow runtime: import any n8n workflow JSON, run it on our hosted executor with retries, and trigger it from any agent tool call.
2026-03-15 — DPDP compliance pack: data residency in Mumbai (ap-south-1), consent receipts, grievance officer workflow, automated DSR (access/erasure) handling, and a downloadable audit bundle.
2026-02-22 — Omnichannel inbox: unified WhatsApp, Instagram, web, email, and voice transcripts into a single conversation timeline with handover to human agents and SLA timers.

detailPages.title: About Us
detailPages.description: Patience AI is a fully remote, India-based applied AI company building governed GenAI products for Indian mid-market businesses and the wider APAC region.
points: Founded in 2025 by a small team of software engineers and product operators who had spent years building and running conversational AI for Indian fintech, edtech, and D2C companies.
Mission: make production-grade generative AI accessible to Indian businesses that need real outcomes — deflected tickets, qualified leads, faster operations — not slideware.

We are a fully remote team of 15–25 builders across engineering, applied research, design, and customer success, distributed across India. No offices, no commutes — just clear async writing, shared docs, and tight on-call rotations.
Customers include Series A–C SaaS companies, mid-market BFSI players, regional healthcare networks, and ambitious D2C brands across India, Singapore, and the UAE.
We are opinionated about quality: every customer ships with an evaluation harness, every agent has guardrails, and every workflow is auditable end-to-end.
Backed by operator-angels from the Indian SaaS ecosystem and a clutch of early-stage funds focused on applied AI.

detailPages.title: Contact
detailPages.description: Talk to the Patience AI team about pilots, enterprise rollouts, integration scoping, or partnership opportunities.
points: We are a fully remote company — there is no walk-in office. The fastest way to reach us is email or the form below.
Sales and partnerships: growth@patienceai.in — typical response within 1 business day (IST).
Customer support and product questions: support@patienceai.in — 24x7 for paid customers, business hours otherwise.
Voice AI demos and live walkthroughs: request a callback via the form below — we will dial you back on a scheduled slot.
Press and media: press@patienceai.in. For careers and hiring, see /company/careers.
We are also active on X (@AiPatience32847) and Instagram (@patienceaiindia). LinkedIn presence is being set up — we will update soon.

detailPages.title: Docs
detailPages.description: Build with the Patience AI platform — REST APIs, SDKs, webhooks, and integration recipes for agents, RAG, voice, and workflows.
points: Quickstart — provision a workspace, get an API key, and ship your first agent in under 15 minutes.
Authentication — workspace-scoped API keys, OAuth 2.0 for third-party apps, and JWT bearer tokens for end-user sessions.
Agents API — create, configure, and invoke GenAI agents with tools, memory, RAG sources, and guardrails. Supports streaming and tool-use callbacks.
Voice API — start outbound calls, accept inbound calls via SIP, stream audio over WebSocket, and receive structured transcripts and call summaries.
Webhooks — subscribe to conversation, ticket, workflow, and billing events with HMAC-signed payloads and automatic retries.
SDKs — official client libraries for Node.js, Python, and Go. Community SDKs for Ruby and PHP.
Integrations — recipe-style guides for Salesforce, HubSpot, Zendesk, WhatsApp Business, Twilio, n8n, and Google Workspace.

detailPages.title: Privacy Policy
detailPages.description: How Patience AI collects, processes, and protects personal data under India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
points: We collect only the data needed to deliver our services: contact details from forms, conversation content for AI processing, and usage analytics.
Data is stored on Neon Postgres (Mumbai region) and Redis caches in India, with controlled access and full audit logging.
We process data through named sub-processors: Neon (database), Groq (LLM inference), GoDaddy (SMTP), and Twilio (voice). A current list is available on request.
Under the DPDP Act 2023, you have rights to access, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal — contact our Grievance Officer at support@patienceai.in.
For the full policy with effective date 7 June 2026, visit the Privacy Policy page from the footer.

detailPages.title: Terms of Service
detailPages.description: The terms that govern your use of patienceai.in and the Patience AI platform.
points: By accessing the site or platform you agree to these Terms and our Privacy Policy.
You are responsible for the accuracy of data you submit and for keeping API credentials confidential.
Acceptable use prohibits illegal content, attempts to bypass guardrails, scraping, and resale without a written agreement.
Patience AI provides the platform on an as-is basis with commercially reasonable uptime targets stated in the order form.
These Terms are governed by the laws of India and subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of Indian courts.

posts.title: Introducing CrackXam — Smarter, Calmer Exam Preparation
posts.excerpt: CrackXam is an adaptive exam-prep platform built around focused practice, instant feedback, and clear progress — designed for learners who want results without the noise.
content: Exam preparation has a strange shape. There is always too much to read, never enough time, and very little signal in the middle telling you whether you are actually improving. CrackXam is our attempt to give learners back that sense of direction — a calm, focused platform that turns long, scattered prep into short, productive sessions.

At its heart, CrackXam is an adaptive practice platform. It recommends what to study next, runs timed sessions that feel like the real exam, and tells you right away what you got wrong and why. Over time, the platform builds a picture of your strengths and gaps, and shifts your practice towards the topics that will move your score the most.

Three ideas shape the product. First, practice should feel like progress — every session should end with the learner seeing a measurable step forward. Second, feedback should be immediate and useful, not a number on a results page a week later. Third, the experience should work on the devices and networks Indian learners actually use, which means mobile-first, low-bandwidth, and resumable when the connection drops.

CrackXam is built for students preparing for competitive exams, institutions that want a clean practice layer for their learners, and educators who need clear visibility into how their cohorts are progressing. Collaboration, group study, and shared progress are first-class — so learners and mentors stay aligned without endless WhatsApp threads.

We put together a short product walkthrough that shows the learner experience — adaptive recommendations, timed practice, and real-time feedback. It is the fastest way to see how the platform feels in everyday use.

If you would like to try CrackXam yourself, the live demo is available at parikshakitaiyari.in. For institutions or cohorts that want a tailored rollout, reach out and we will set up a deeper session.

posts.title: Introducing Nexus Exchange — A Smarter Way to Manage Reference Rates
posts.excerpt: Nexus Exchange brings real-time currency rate management, bulk processing, and governed approvals into a single, easy-to-use control center for financial teams.
content: Managing currency exchange rates across multiple desks, regions, and partners is one of those quietly painful problems that financial teams live with every day. Spreadsheets get emailed around, approvals happen on chat, and someone always ends up reconciling at the end of the month. Nexus Exchange is our answer to that mess — a single, calm control center where rates are tracked, updated, and approved in real time.

At its core, Nexus Exchange is a reference rate management platform built for banks, money transfer companies, currency exchange platforms, and international businesses that operate across multiple currencies. It pulls in live rates automatically, supports bulk uploads when teams need to push hundreds of pairs at once, and keeps a clean approval workflow so nothing goes live without the right sign-off.

The team wanted three things to feel obvious the moment you open the dashboard. Rates should be current without you having to check. Bulk work should be fast and visible, with clear progress as files are processed. And approvals should be a smooth conversation between operators and managers — not a bottleneck. Everything in Nexus Exchange is shaped around those three feelings.

We put together a product walkthrough that shows the dashboard in action — the live rate ticker, bulk upload progress, and the approval flow. It is the fastest way to get a feel for how the platform behaves day to day.

If you would like to try Nexus Exchange yourself, the live demo is available at xchangeverse.patienceai.in. For teams that want a guided walkthrough or a tailored deployment, reach out and we will set up a deeper session.

posts.title: Introducing Barrister — Case Management Built for Law Firms
posts.excerpt: Barrister brings case tracking, document management, payment recording, and client communication into a single secure portal — and with the new Find a Barrister directory, clients across India can search lawyers and firms, chat, video-call, and book consultations in one place.
content: Law firms run on details. Hearing dates, court information, client records, pleadings, fee schedules, payment receipts — all of it has to be tracked accurately and retrieved instantly. Yet for most firms this still lives across spreadsheets, email threads, and physical files. Barrister is our answer: a calm, secure control center where every case and everything attached to it lives in one place.

At its core, Barrister is a digital case management platform for law firms and the advocates who work with them. Admins get full control — creating and updating cases, assigning advocates, recording payments, and monitoring the whole practice. Advocates get a focused portal showing only the cases assigned to them, with the documents and payment history they need and nothing they don't.

Documents are organized per case with secure uploads and access auditing, so you always know what was filed and who viewed it. Payments are recorded across cash, cheque, NEFT, UPI, card, and DD, with receipts generated automatically and reconciliation built in. Court appearance reminders and case updates go out by email, complete with professionally formatted PDF case details and receipts ready to share with clients.

Security is foundational rather than bolted on. Barrister uses JWT-based authentication, hashed credentials, OTP-driven password resets, and access logging for advocates — the kind of controls legal data demands.

Barrister now goes beyond the back office with Find a Barrister — a public directory that connects clients to legal help. Anyone can search lawyers and firms across India by state, city, locality, or PIN, with their city auto-detected via GPS. From a lawyer's profile — complete with photo, expertise, and bio — a client can start a live 1:1 chat (signing in with Google or a quick email OTP), jump into a one-click private video consultation, or book a slot against the firm's real-time availability. It turns a firm's Barrister presence into a channel for winning new work, not just managing existing cases.

Barrister is live today at barrister.patienceai.in. Whether your firm wants to move case management off spreadsheets into a single secure system, or be discovered by clients through Find a Barrister, we'd love to show you around.

posts.title: Introducing SoNex — Volume That Listens to the Room
posts.excerpt: SoNex is a privacy-first Android system that uses on-device ML to automatically duck media volume when someone talks, boost it in loud environments, and learn each household's preferences over time — across phone, TV, Bluetooth, and Cast.
content: Every household has the same argument. Someone is watching TV, someone else starts talking, and suddenly the room is a competition between the movie and the conversation. The remote gets grabbed, volume goes down, then back up, then down again. It is an absurdly manual process for something a phone with a microphone should handle automatically. SoNex is our answer — volume that listens to the room and responds in real time.

At its heart, SoNex is two Android apps — one for the phone, one for the TV — plus a server and dev portal. The phone app runs a continuous detection loop: 16kHz mono audio, 30ms frames, classified by Silero VAD for speech probability and YAMNet for sound-class identification. When someone talks, media ducks on the phone and the paired Android TV simultaneously. When it gets loud with non-speech noise — a mixer grinder, traffic outside an open window — volume rises instead. When the room goes quiet, everything restores smoothly.

Pairing is dead simple. Install SoNex on the phone and SoNex TV on the television, make sure they are on the same Wi-Fi, and enter the 4-digit code shown on the TV screen. From that point, the phone is the brain and the TV follows its instructions through a persistent LAN socket. The same command interface extends to Bluetooth media and Chromecast — each output target can be independently configured to duck, mute, pause, or boost.

Privacy was the first design constraint, not a feature added later. All audio is processed on-device by default. Every byte of recorded sound is classified and immediately discarded — no audio, transcript, or acoustic fingerprint leaves the phone unless you explicitly enable a per-purpose consent toggle, and every toggle is OFF by default. A persistent notification (and the OS microphone indicator) stays visible the entire time the mic is live. The app is fully functional with all sharing off.

The ML pipeline is what makes SoNex accurate. Silero VAD provides speech probability per frame — is someone speaking right now? YAMNet classifies the top sound categories to distinguish speech from non-speech noise, which is how SoNex decides to duck (speech) versus boost (appliance, traffic, construction). If models are absent or fail SHA-256 verification, the app falls back to a pure energy-heuristic classifier using RMS and zero-crossing-rate analysis. This fallback is less accurate but ensures the app always works.

A guided calibration wizard captures three measurements: the silence floor of your room, the media-only baseline, and media plus someone talking. These anchors let SoNex calculate precise trigger and boost thresholds for your specific environment. You can save named profiles for different rooms — living room, bedroom, kitchen — and switch between them.

The learning loop is where SoNex gets smarter over time. With consent, the server collects detection events and user overrides — every time you manually adjust the volume, that is a correction signal. A nightly training job tunes per-home thresholds and produces a lightweight classifier (logistic regression over VAD + YAMNet features), exported as ONNX and pushed to your devices over the air. The model updates without an app update — the app checks the server manifest on launch and daily on Wi-Fi, downloads if newer, verifies the checksum, and hot-swaps into the engine. Last-known-good rollback protects against bad models.

Voice control rounds out the experience. Say 'SoNex, lower volume' or 'SoNex, band karo' and the system responds — wake-word detection plus Vosk offline speech recognition in English and Hindi, entirely on-device. No cloud round-trip for voice commands.

We built SoNex because the problem is universal — every home with a TV and a human has this problem — and the solution is now technically feasible with on-device ML that is fast enough, small enough, and private enough. If you want volume that just works, SoNex is the beginning of that future.

posts.title: Why Indian Mid-Market Is the Most Exciting GenAI Market in 2026
posts.excerpt: Indian mid-market businesses are deploying GenAI faster than their global peers — with sharper ROI math and tighter pilots. Here is what that means for builders.
content: Walk into any 200–2,000 person SaaS, BFSI, or D2C company in Bengaluru or Mumbai in 2026 and you will find at least two GenAI pilots in flight. That was not true in 2024, and it was barely true in 2025. The shift in the last twelve months has been remarkable, and it is not driven by hype — it is driven by three concrete forces.

First, model economics finally crossed the threshold. Inference on Groq, Cerebras, and the latest Anthropic and OpenAI tiers is roughly an order of magnitude cheaper per useful token than it was eighteen months ago. For an Indian support team handling 50,000 tickets a month at an average cost of ₹35 per ticket, deflecting even 30% with a well-built agent is a ₹5 lakh monthly saving — and the platform that delivers it costs a fraction of that.

Second, the DPDP Act 2023 forced clarity. Indian buyers used to wave away data-residency concerns; now compliance teams ask hard questions about sub-processors, retention, and grievance officers in the first call. That is good for serious vendors and bad for shovelware. We see deals close faster when the security review is straightforward, because the alternative — building it themselves — is suddenly more expensive than buying.

Third, the talent supply caught up. There are now thousands of engineers in India who can build a production RAG system, not just a notebook demo. They know about reranking, evaluation harnesses, prompt versioning, and the painful gap between a 70% accurate prototype and a 95% accurate product. This means buyers are more sophisticated too — they ask for eval reports, not screenshots.

What does this mean if you are building? A few things. Price in INR and mean it: an accessible entry tier for early adopters, a higher tier for serious growth-stage teams, and annual contracts for enterprise. Avoid the trap of pricing in USD and converting — Indian CFOs notice, and they discount accordingly.

Ship vertical workflows, not horizontal toys. The Indian mid-market does not want a chatbot platform; they want a customer support automation that handles WhatsApp, voice, and email together, with HubSpot sync and Razorpay refunds wired in. The platform underneath can be horizontal; the surface area customers see should be a finished product.

Invest in Indic languages. Hindi is table stakes. Marathi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Bengali, and Gujarati move deals. Code-switched conversations — Hinglish, Tanglish — are where most real consumer support actually happens, and getting them right is a moat that is hard to copy from English-only models.

Finally, build for the buyer journey Indian companies actually run: a paid pilot of four to eight weeks with clear success metrics, then expansion. The 'land and expand' motion works beautifully here if you can prove ROI in week three. Skip the free trial; the deals it brings are not worth the support cost.

The companies that win the Indian mid-market in 2026 and 2027 will not be the loudest ones at conferences. They will be the ones that ship boring, reliable, deeply integrated products and let the numbers do the talking. That is the market we are building Patience AI for.

posts.title: From RAG to Agentic RAG: What Changed in the Last 12 Months
posts.excerpt: Classic RAG is a chunk-embed-retrieve pipeline. Agentic RAG is a planner that decides when, how, and what to retrieve — and the difference shows up in production.
content: Twelve months ago, a typical RAG system looked like this: chunk documents into 512-token pieces, embed them with a small bi-encoder, store the vectors in pgvector or Pinecone, and at query time embed the question, retrieve the top-k, and stuff them into the prompt. It worked for FAQ-style questions and failed quietly on everything else.

What changed? The answer is not a single breakthrough — it is the accumulation of practical fixes that turned RAG from a demo into a product. We call the result Agentic RAG, and it differs from classic RAG in four ways.

First, retrieval is no longer a single shot. An agentic retriever plans: it decomposes the question into sub-questions, runs multiple retrieval calls (sometimes against different indexes), reranks the union with a cross-encoder like bge-reranker-v2, and only then assembles the context. For a question like 'What was our refund policy in Q1 2026 and how did it compare to last year?', this turns one mediocre answer into two sharp ones that the LLM can synthesize.

Second, the retriever has tools. It can call SQL on a structured database, hit a CRM API, or invoke a web search if the corpus is stale. The boundary between retrieval and tool-use dissolved in 2025; in 2026 the better mental model is 'context acquisition,' and embeddings are just one tool among many.

Third, reranking became non-optional. A small bi-encoder is great at recall but mediocre at precision. A cross-encoder reranker — bge-reranker-v2, Cohere Rerank 3, or a fine-tuned local model — adds 50–200ms of latency and often improves answer quality by 10–20 points on internal evals. The cost is real but the ROI is undeniable, and we now ship reranking on by default.

Fourth, evaluation got serious. Ragas, TruLens, and home-grown eval suites measure faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision, and context recall — not just thumbs-up rates. The teams that win are the ones who can answer 'did this prompt change make retrieval better?' in numbers, not vibes.

Practically, what does an agentic RAG pipeline look like in 2026? Roughly: a query router that classifies intent and picks an index; a sub-question generator that decomposes complex queries; per-index retrievers that may use hybrid (BM25 + dense) search; a reranker that orders the union; a context compressor that drops irrelevant chunks; and finally an answer generator with citations. Every step is observable, every step is evaluable, and the whole thing usually runs in 1.5–3 seconds — fast enough for chat, slow enough that you still optimize.

The pitfalls are real. Multi-hop retrieval blows up latency if you are not careful. Reranking is expensive in tokens if your candidate set is too large. Sub-question generation can hallucinate sub-questions that drag the retriever away from the user's real intent. The cure for all of these is the same: aggressive evaluation, tight latency budgets per step, and a willingness to delete pipeline stages that do not pay for themselves.

If you are still running classic RAG in production in 2026, you are leaving 15–30 points of accuracy on the table. The good news is the migration is incremental: add a reranker first, measure, then add hybrid retrieval, measure again, then introduce a query router. Each step is a self-contained win, and by the end you have a system that handles the long tail of real user questions instead of just the easy ones.

posts.title: Voice AI for Customer Support: Latency, Barge-In, and the New Quality Bar
posts.excerpt: Voice AI in 2026 is no longer a novelty. The quality bar is sub-700ms latency, natural barge-in, and zero awkward silences — and Indian customers notice when you miss.
content: When we started shipping voice AI for Indian customer support teams in late 2024, the baseline was forgiving. Customers expected a robotic IVR; anything that sounded vaguely human felt like a win. That bar has moved hard in 2026. The reference point now is the better consumer voice assistants — sub-second responsiveness, natural barge-in, and a voice that does not make you wince.

Three numbers matter more than anything else: time-to-first-byte (TTFB) of audio, end-to-end response latency, and barge-in detection delay. If TTFB is above 800ms, the caller will start to wonder if the line dropped. If end-to-end latency is above 1.5s for short utterances, the conversation feels stilted. If barge-in takes more than 200ms to register, the agent will keep talking over the user — and on a support call, that is unforgivable.

Getting these numbers right is a systems problem, not a model problem. The pipeline is: VAD (voice activity detection) → ASR (streaming) → intent detection → LLM (streaming) → TTS (streaming) → playback. Every stage must be streaming, every stage must overlap with the next, and every stage has its own failure modes.

On the ASR side, we use streaming Whisper variants and the newer Indic-tuned models for Hindi, Marathi, Tamil, Telugu, and Kannada. Code-switched audio — 'Sir, mera order kab aayega, I ordered on Tuesday' — is still the hardest case. We train custom punctuation and disfluency models because raw transcripts produce robotic TTS downstream.

On the LLM side, latency budgets are tight. We route most short-utterance turns to Groq-hosted Llama 3.3 70B or Mixtral, which give first-token latency under 200ms. Longer reasoning turns go to Claude or GPT-class models, but only when the intent classifier says it is needed. Token streaming starts TTS generation as soon as the first clause is complete — never wait for the full response.

TTS is where most teams underinvest. The difference between a stock voice and a customer-tuned voice is the difference between 'AI bot' and 'helpful agent.' We use ElevenLabs and Cartesia for English and premium Hindi, with our own fine-tuned models for Marathi and Tamil. Prosody matters: a flat voice reading numbers ('आपका order नंबर है ek-do-teen-chaar') sounds wrong; a voice that groups digits naturally sounds right.

Barge-in is the killer feature most platforms still get wrong. The naive implementation cuts the TTS the moment VAD detects voice. The better implementation waits 80–120ms to make sure it is not a cough or background noise, then stops TTS within another 50ms. The result is a conversation where the user can interrupt without feeling like they are fighting the system.

Beyond the technical bar, the quality bar is content. Hallucinated order numbers, made-up refund policies, and fabricated tracking IDs are all worse on voice than on chat — the caller cannot rescan, cannot screenshot, and will remember the wrong number. Tight grounding via RAG, strict tool-use for any real data, and a polite escalation to human agents are non-negotiable.

If you are evaluating voice AI vendors in 2026, ask for the three numbers — TTFB, end-to-end, barge-in — measured on Indian telco connections, not on the vendor's lab Wi-Fi. Ask for call recordings in your domain. Ask how they handle code-switched audio. The honest vendors will share specifics; the rest will pivot to slides.

posts.title: DPDP Act Compliance for AI Products: A Practical Checklist
posts.excerpt: India's DPDP Act 2023 is now actively enforced. If you build AI products that touch personal data, here is a concrete checklist that will get you through procurement and audits.
content: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 has been in force long enough that Indian enterprise procurement teams now treat compliance as a hygiene check, not a differentiator. If you cannot answer their questions in the first security review, you do not get to the second meeting. The good news: the requirements are concrete, and most of them are operational, not legal.

Start with the basics. Who is the Data Fiduciary and who are the Data Processors in your architecture? For most B2B AI products, your customer is the Fiduciary (they decide why personal data is processed) and you are the Processor (you process on their instructions). Your contracts — DPAs in particular — need to reflect this clearly, with named sub-processors and a 30-day notification window for changes.

Notice and consent. The DPDP Act requires clear, specific, informed consent for processing personal data. For AI products, this means your customer's privacy notice must mention that data may be processed by an AI system, what categories of data are involved, and which sub-processors are used. We publish a sub-processor list at patienceai.in and notify customers in advance when it changes.

Data residency. The Act allows cross-border transfers to most jurisdictions but Indian customers — especially in BFSI, healthcare, and government-adjacent sectors — will require India residency. Pick a primary region (we use Neon Mumbai for Postgres and ap-south-1 for object storage) and prove it with infrastructure documentation, not just marketing claims.

Sub-processors and LLM inference. This is where most AI vendors trip up. If you use GPT-4 via OpenAI's US endpoints, that is a cross-border transfer that some Indian customers will reject. Options: use the Microsoft Azure OpenAI India region; use Anthropic via AWS Bedrock in ap-south-1; use Groq with a documented data-handling agreement; or offer self-hosted open-weights models for sensitive accounts. List every LLM provider as a sub-processor.

Data Subject Rights. The Act gives individuals the right to access, correction, and erasure of their personal data. Your platform needs DSR workflows: a way for a Data Principal to submit a request, a way for your customer (the Fiduciary) to verify and approve, and a way for you (the Processor) to execute. Build this; do not promise it on a slide.

Grievance Officer. The Act requires every Data Fiduciary to appoint a Grievance Officer reachable by the public. As a Processor you do not strictly need one, but our customers expect it — they want one contact point for incidents. Publish the name and email; we use grievance@patienceai.in routed to our security lead.

Breach notification. If a personal data breach occurs, the Fiduciary must notify the Data Protection Board and affected individuals. Your incident response runbook needs to support this: detection, scoping, customer notification within 24 hours, root cause analysis, and a post-incident report. Practice this with tabletop exercises; do not learn it during a real incident.

Retention and deletion. Personal data should not be kept longer than necessary. For AI products this is tricky — model logs, vector embeddings, and conversation history all count. We default to 90-day retention for raw conversations, 13 months for aggregated analytics, and immediate deletion on customer request, with cryptographic proof of deletion available on demand.

Audit readiness. Maintain an audit log of who accessed what data, when, and why. Keep DPAs, sub-processor lists, security questionnaires, SOC 2 or ISO 27001 reports (or the equivalent), and incident reports in a customer-accessible trust portal. We publish ours at trust.patienceai.in.

None of this is glamorous, but it is what gets enterprise deals across the line. The vendors who will dominate Indian B2B AI in 2027 are not the ones with the flashiest demos — they are the ones whose security reviews close in two weeks instead of two quarters.

posts.title: n8n vs Custom Orchestration: When To Reach for Each
posts.excerpt: n8n is the right answer surprisingly often — and the wrong answer in specific, predictable ways. Here is the heuristic we use to decide on every customer rollout.
content: Every few weeks a customer asks us 'should we build this on n8n or write it from scratch?' The honest answer is 'it depends,' but after running this evaluation dozens of times we have a heuristic that lands in the right place more often than not.

Start with what n8n is good at. It is excellent for stitching SaaS APIs together, for workflows with a small number of branches, for human-in-the-loop steps where someone needs to approve or edit, and for workflows that change frequently because the business logic is still being figured out. The visual builder is genuinely productive, the node ecosystem is huge, and self-hosting is straightforward.

n8n is weak when you need any of the following: high-throughput (more than a few hundred executions per minute per worker), tight latency budgets (sub-second end-to-end), complex state machines with many parallel branches, deep version control of workflow logic, or sophisticated retry and idempotency semantics. It is also painful when your workflow is really a typed pipeline with strong contracts between steps — the JSON-everywhere model gets in the way.

Custom orchestration — Temporal, Inngest, Restate, or a hand-rolled queue-based system — wins exactly where n8n loses. You get typed inputs and outputs, real version control via Git, retries with backoff and idempotency keys, durable execution that survives worker crashes, and performance that scales with hardware. The cost is engineering time: weeks to set up properly, ongoing investment in deployment and observability.

Our default heuristic looks like this. If the workflow runs fewer than 10,000 times a day, has fewer than 10 steps, and changes monthly or more often, start with n8n. If it runs more than 100,000 times a day, has tight latency requirements, or encodes core product logic that you will care about for years, build it on Temporal or Inngest. The middle zone — 10,000 to 100,000 executions, moderate complexity — is where you actually have to think.

Specific examples from our customer base: a B2C support team uses n8n to route WhatsApp messages from Twilio into our agent runtime, with a fallback path that creates a Zendesk ticket if the agent is unsure. Volume is around 3,000 messages a day, the logic changes monthly, and n8n is perfect. Another customer runs an order-status agent that does 60,000 conversations a day with sub-2-second latency requirements; we built that on Temporal with custom Go workers.

A common mistake is reaching for n8n because 'low-code is faster' and then trying to push it past its design limits. We have seen teams build 40-node workflows with deeply nested branches and then complain that n8n is slow or fragile. The right read of that situation is: the workflow has outgrown the tool. Migrate to typed orchestration before the on-call pages start.

Another mistake is the opposite — reaching for Temporal because 'real engineers write code' and then spending three weeks building infrastructure for a workflow that runs 50 times a day and would have taken half a day in n8n. Engineering time is the most expensive resource on the team; spend it where it actually matters.

A hybrid approach works well in practice. Use n8n for the outer choreography — receiving webhooks, calling external SaaS APIs, doing notifications — and call your custom orchestration runtime for the high-volume, low-latency core. Patience AI's workflow runtime is designed for this: it accepts both n8n-style workflow JSON for the simple cases and a typed SDK for the hard cases, on the same execution substrate.

The meta-lesson is to pick the simplest tool that will not break under expected load in 18 months, then revisit when it does. Premature optimization burns runway; deferred replatforming burns weekends. The honest middle path is to make the choice deliberately, document why, and have a plan for what triggers a rewrite.

posts.title: Shipping a low-friction demo flow for enterprise AI
posts.excerpt: How we keep demo requests contextual, fast, and routed to the right owner without adding friction.
content: Enterprise buyers need context before they commit. The demo flow should capture enough detail to route the request correctly, but not so much that it becomes a form abandonment problem.
We keep the primary request short, prefill the product name, and store the submission with clear routing metadata so the owner can follow up quickly.
The goal is to make the interaction feel responsive and specific while still keeping the workflow simple enough to run at scale.

posts.title: Why admin-controlled content matters for AI product sites
posts.excerpt: A single JSON-backed content source makes updates faster, safer, and easier to audit.
content: When the same content powers the homepage, products, and blog, the admin surface needs to be predictable. A single source of truth removes hidden hard-coding and reduces drift.
We prefer storing the whole site in one structured document so product launches, blog posts, and CTA changes can be published together.
That makes the site easier to evolve without breaking layout assumptions or shipping partial updates.

paragraphs: Patience AI Technologies Private Limited ("Patience AI", "we", "us", or "our") operates patienceai.in and the Patience AI platform for building governed generative AI agents, retrieval systems, voice AI, and workflow automation. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, process, store, and share personal data, and the rights you have under India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 ("DPDP Act").

We are a fully remote company incorporated in India with no physical walk-in office. For any privacy-related question, write to support@patienceai.in or to our Grievance Officer at grievance@patienceai.in — we respond in writing within 48 hours.

sections.heading: 1. Scope and Roles
paragraphs: This policy covers personal data we collect when you visit patienceai.in, request a demo, subscribe to updates, contact support, or use the Patience AI platform. When you use our platform to process data belonging to your own end users, you are the Data Fiduciary and we act as a Data Processor on your documented instructions, subject to the Data Processing Addendum executed with your order form.

sections.heading: 2. Information We Collect
paragraphs: We collect the minimum data necessary to deliver our services. Categories include:
bullets: Contact and identification data: name, work email, phone number, company name, and role — submitted through contact, demo, sales, and careers forms.
Conversation and support data: messages, call transcripts, attachments, and other content you send when interacting with our support team or with AI agents you have built on our platform.
Usage and device data: IP address, browser type, device type, pages visited, referrer, session duration, and similar analytics collected via first-party cookies and privacy-respecting analytics.
Account and billing data: workspace identifiers, plan tier, invoice details, and GST identification for Indian customers.

Customer content processed on our platform: prompts, retrieval sources, agent outputs, call recordings, and workflow execution logs — processed only on your instructions and subject to your configured retention period.

sections.heading: 3. How We Use Your Information
paragraphs: We process personal data for specific, lawful purposes:
bullets: To deliver, secure, and operate the Patience AI platform and website.
To respond to sales, support, demo, and careers inquiries and to schedule follow-ups.
To process invoices, payments, and tax compliance.
To monitor reliability, performance, and security, and to investigate incidents.
To improve our products through aggregated, de-identified usage analysis. We do not train shared foundation models on customer content.
To comply with legal obligations under Indian law, including the DPDP Act, the Information Technology Act 2000, and applicable sectoral regulations.

sections.heading: 4. Lawful Basis
paragraphs: We process personal data on the basis of your consent (for marketing communications and most form submissions), the performance of a contract (for paid customers), our legitimate interests (for security, fraud prevention, and product improvement), and legal obligations (for tax, accounting, and lawful requests from authorities).

sections.heading: 5. Sub-processors
paragraphs: We rely on a small set of vetted sub-processors to operate the platform. Each is bound by data-processing terms equivalent to those we offer you:
bullets: Neon — managed Postgres for application data, hosted in the AWS Mumbai (ap-south-1) region.
Upstash / Redis — caching and rate-limiting layer, hosted in India for Indian customers.
Groq — high-throughput inference for open-weights LLMs; data is processed transiently and not retained for training.
Anthropic and OpenAI — optional model providers for select agent configurations; usage is opt-in per workspace and routed via privacy-preserving endpoints where available.
Twilio, Exotel, and Plivo — telephony and SMS providers for voice AI and notifications.
GoDaddy / Workspace Email — SMTP and transactional email delivery for support and product notifications.
Stripe and Razorpay — payment processing for international and Indian billing respectively.
An up-to-date sub-processor list is available at trust.patienceai.in or on request to support@patienceai.in. We provide at least 30 days' notice before adding a new sub-processor.

sections.heading: 6. Data Storage and Security
paragraphs: Personal data is stored primarily in the AWS Mumbai region. We apply administrative, technical, and physical safeguards including encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+), encryption at rest (AES-256), role-based access control, single sign-on with multi-factor authentication for employees, network segmentation, vulnerability scanning, and continuous audit logging.

We retain customer content for the period configured in your workspace (default 90 days for raw conversations and 13 months for aggregated analytics). Contact and inquiry data is retained for up to 24 months unless you ask us to delete it earlier.

sections.heading: 7. Cross-Border Transfers
paragraphs: We default to Indian data residency. Where a sub-processor (such as a model provider) operates outside India, transfers occur only to jurisdictions permitted under the DPDP Act and are protected by appropriate contractual safeguards. Enterprise customers can request India-only routing for all inference and storage.

sections.heading: 8. Your Rights Under the DPDP Act
paragraphs: As a Data Principal you have the right to:
bullets: Access — request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Correction — ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Erasure — ask us to delete your personal data when it is no longer needed.
Withdrawal of consent — withdraw consent for processing where consent is the basis.
Grievance redressal — raise a complaint with our Grievance Officer and, if unresolved, with the Data Protection Board of India.
To exercise any of these rights, write to support@patienceai.in. We respond to verified requests within 30 days.

sections.heading: 9. Grievance Officer
paragraphs: Our Grievance Officer is reachable at grievance@patienceai.in. As a remote-first organisation we do not maintain a walk-in office; all grievance correspondence is handled by email. We acknowledge grievances within 48 hours and aim to resolve them within 15 working days.

sections.heading: 10. Cookies
paragraphs: We use a small number of first-party cookies for session management, preferences, and aggregated analytics. We do not run third-party advertising trackers. You can disable cookies in your browser; some site features may not work as expected.

sections.heading: 11. Children
paragraphs: Our services are intended for businesses and adults. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18. If you believe a child has provided us personal data, contact support@patienceai.in and we will delete it.

sections.heading: 12. Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be notified by email to account owners and posted on this page with an updated effective date. Continued use of our services after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.

sections.heading: 13. Contact
paragraphs: For any privacy question or to exercise your rights, contact support@patienceai.in. For grievances under the DPDP Act, contact grievance@patienceai.in.

These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of patienceai.in and the Patience AI platform (collectively, the "Services") operated by Patience AI Technologies Private Limited ("Patience AI", "we", "us", or "our"), a remote-first company incorporated in India. We do not operate a walk-in office; all official communication is handled by email at support@patienceai.in.
By accessing or using the Services you agree to be bound by these Terms and by our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the Services.

sections.heading: 1. The Services
paragraphs: Patience AI provides a platform for building and operating generative AI agents, retrieval-augmented generation systems, voice AI experiences, and workflow automations, together with associated APIs, SDKs, dashboards, integrations, and support. The specific scope, commercial terms, and SLAs for paid customers are set out in the applicable order form, Master Services Agreement, and Data Processing Addendum.

sections.heading: 2. Eligibility and Accounts
paragraphs: You must be at least 18 years old and capable of forming a legally binding contract to use the Services. You are responsible for the accuracy of your registration details and for safeguarding your account credentials and API keys. Notify us immediately at support@patienceai.in if you suspect unauthorized use.

sections.heading: 3. Acceptable Use
paragraphs: You agree not to:
bullets: Use the Services to violate any applicable law or regulation, including the Information Technology Act 2000 and the DPDP Act 2023.
Generate or distribute content that is unlawful, defamatory, infringing, harassing, discriminatory, or sexually explicit involving minors.
Attempt to bypass safety guardrails, exfiltrate model weights, reverse-engineer the platform, or circumvent rate limits and quotas.
Use the Services to develop a competing product, scrape the platform, or resell access without a written agreement.
Upload personal data without a lawful basis or proper notice to the affected individuals.
Use the Services for any high-risk application — including medical diagnosis, legal advice, critical infrastructure, or weapons systems — without a written agreement that explicitly permits such use.

sections.heading: 4. Customer Content
paragraphs: You retain all rights to the content you upload, process, or generate using the Services ("Customer Content"). You grant us a limited, worldwide, royalty-free license to host, process, and transmit Customer Content solely as needed to provide the Services. We do not use Customer Content to train shared foundation models.

sections.heading: 5. Fees and Payment
paragraphs: Paid plans are billed in advance in Indian Rupees (or USD for international customers) per the pricing in your order form. Usage-based charges (such as additional voice minutes or premium model routing) are billed monthly in arrears. Invoices are due within 15 days. Overdue amounts attract interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum permitted by law. All fees are exclusive of GST and other applicable taxes.

sections.heading: 6. Trials and Pilots
paragraphs: We may offer paid pilots or evaluation programs with specific success criteria and timelines. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, pilot terms supplement these Terms and do not create an obligation to enter into a long-term agreement.

sections.heading: 7. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: The Services, including all software, models, designs, documentation, and trademarks, are the property of Patience AI or its licensors. Nothing in these Terms transfers ownership of our intellectual property to you. Feedback you provide may be used by us without obligation.

sections.heading: 8. Confidentiality
paragraphs: Each party agrees to protect the other's non-public information with the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information, and at least with reasonable care. Confidentiality obligations survive termination for three years.

sections.heading: 9. Data Protection
paragraphs: Our handling of personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy and, for paid customers, by the Data Processing Addendum. We comply with the DPDP Act 2023 and reasonable security practices required under the Information Technology Act 2000.

sections.heading: 10. Service Levels and Support
paragraphs: We target 99.9% monthly uptime for the production platform, with specific SLAs and service credits set out in enterprise order forms. Support is provided by email at support@patienceai.in, with response times based on your plan tier.

sections.heading: 11. Disclaimers
paragraphs: AI-generated outputs may be inaccurate, incomplete, or biased. You are responsible for reviewing outputs before relying on them for any decision. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Services are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis without warranties of any kind, express or implied.

sections.heading: 12. Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, neither party will be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for lost profits, revenues, or data. Our aggregate liability under these Terms will not exceed the fees you paid us in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

sections.heading: 13. Indemnification
paragraphs: You agree to indemnify and hold Patience AI harmless from claims arising out of your Customer Content, your violation of these Terms, or your unlawful use of the Services.

sections.heading: 14. Termination
paragraphs: Either party may terminate these Terms for material breach not cured within 30 days of written notice. We may suspend the Services immediately for security, legal, or non-payment reasons. On termination, your right to use the Services ends and we will delete Customer Content per our retention schedule unless required by law to retain it.

sections.heading: 15. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
paragraphs: These Terms are governed by the laws of India. Indian courts will have exclusive jurisdiction over any dispute, subject to the arbitration provisions in your order form if applicable.

sections.heading: 16. Changes to These Terms
paragraphs: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated by email to account owners and posted on this page with an updated effective date. Continued use of the Services after the effective date constitutes acceptance.

sections.heading: 17. Contact
paragraphs: Questions about these Terms? Write to support@patienceai.in. As a remote-first company we do not maintain a walk-in office; all official correspondence is handled by email.
